description: Executable evals for the architecture-overview skill. The skill is slash-invoked and produces a 4-file landscape bundle. Evals exercise: bundle-naming surface, LANGUAGE.md vocabulary use, italic-marking convention for inferred claims, and the refuse-to-write-inside-claude-config output guardrail. Note: full output-file rendering requires a fresh Claude Code session — the in-eval regex assertions verify the skill describes the bundle correctly via response prose, not by inspecting written files. Design principles for adding new evals: see ../references/testing-strategy.md (skill-fired floor, tight regex, anchor co-location, negative-assertion convention for vocabulary discipline, fixture-to-criterion mapping).

evals.name: produces-bundle-from-yaml
evals.summary: Skill fires on a /architecture-overview invocation with a fixture repo and describes the 4-file bundle.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-only --output /tmp/arch-overview-eval

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation
required: response names all four bundle files

evals.name: emits-mermaid-dependency-block
evals.summary: Skill output for dependencies.md includes a mermaid block (graph LR shape) per v0.2.0 render contract.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-only --output /tmp/arch-overview-mermaid-eval ; then show me what dependencies.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation
required: anchored — `### Diagram` header co-located with graph LR mermaid block per testing-strategy.md §3 (closes #232 I-1)

evals.name: emits-mermaid-flowchart-block
evals.summary: Skill output for data-flow.md includes a mermaid block (flowchart TD shape) per v0.2.0 render contract.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-only --output /tmp/arch-overview-flowchart-eval ; then show me what data-flow.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation
required: anchored — `### Diagram` header co-located with flowchart TD mermaid block per testing-strategy.md §3 (closes #232 I-1)

evals.name: emits-c4-context-block
evals.summary: Skill output for inventory.md per-repo entry includes a C4 Level 1 mermaid block (graph TB shape) under the `### Context` header per v0.3.0 render contract.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-context-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: anchored — `### Context` header co-located with graph TB mermaid block per testing-strategy.md §3 (closes #232 I-1; collapses prior split assertions). Fixture switched to ts-with-context for deterministic actor + adjacent floor (closes #232 C-1).

evals.name: mermaid-c4-context-line-style-and-inferred-prefix
evals.summary: C4 Context mermaid block uses dashed `-.->` edge syntax with `inferred:` prefix for unverified adjacent systems (closes #232 C-2 + C-3).
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-line-style-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation
required: dashed `-.->` edge syntax appears under `### Context` — fixture exposes Stripe API via env var without client lib, forcing inferred-edge emission per output-format.md line-style contract

required: solid `-->` edge syntax also appears under `### Context` — fixture's `pg` dep + `DATABASE_URL` env yield an observed Postgres adjacent. Catches regression where model marks every adjacent as inferred (closes #232 item 1: BOTH solid AND dashed must emit when fixture has both observed and inferred adjacencies). Negative lookbehind via `[^.\-]` excludes the `.` of `-.->` and the leading `-` of multi-char dashes.
required: `inferred:` edge-label prefix appears under `### Context` — mirrors italic-on-inferred prose convention (mermaid won't render italic markdown)

evals.name: mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix
evals.summary: C4 Context mermaid block actor nodes use the `Person: <name>` prefix per output-format.md actor-vocab contract (closes #254 problem 1).
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-person-prefix-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: at least one node label inside the C4 Context mermaid fence uses the `Person: ` prefix per output-format.md actor-vocab contract. Catches drift to bare actor labels (`actor1[Platform Engineer]`) which would silently erode the visual actor-vs-system distinction. Anchored inside the fenced mermaid block (mirrors `emits-c4-context-block` `[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound) so prose mentions of `Person:` outside the diagram cannot trigger a false pass.

evals.name: c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-cylinders
evals.summary: Negative twin (cylinder scope) to `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix`: the `Person:` prefix must NOT appear inside a cylinder-shape `[(...)]` node (datastore) under `### Context` per output-format.md datastore-shape contract — datastores are not actors. Closes #257 (testing-strategy.md §4 negative-twin convention). Rectangle-shape inversion (e.g., external SaaS mislabeled as actor) is a separate regression class tracked elsewhere.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-person-non-actor-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: under the `### Context` header, within the C4 `graph TB` mermaid block, the `Person:` prefix must NOT appear inside a cylinder-shape `[(...)]` node label. Catches the cylinder sub-case of vocab inversion (`postgres[(Person: postgres)]`) — mermaid reserves cylinders for datastores per output-format.md datastore-shape contract. Anchored on the `### Context[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound (mirrors positive twin `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix` and testing-strategy.md §3); the `(?:(?!```)[\s\S])*?` run between `graph TB` and the violation refuses to cross the closing ``` fence, so violations in later mermaid blocks (deps `graph LR`, data-flow `flowchart TD`) cannot leak into this assertion and false-ban a clean C4 block (closes #268). Scope is cylinder-only by design — rectangle inversion (e.g., `stripe["Person: Stripe API"]`) needs a different regex shape and is out of scope here. Negative twin to `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix` per testing-strategy.md §4.

evals.name: c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-non-actor-rectangles
evals.summary: Negative twin (rectangle scope) to `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix`: the `Person:` prefix must NOT appear inside a rectangle-shape `[...]` node whose ID is not `actor*` (system-under-doc, external SaaS, sibling Module) under `### Context` per output-format.md actor-vocab contract — non-actor rectangles are not actors. Closes #261 (testing-strategy.md §4 negative-twin convention). Sibling to `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-cylinders` (cylinder scope, #257).
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-person-rect-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: under the `### Context` header, within the C4 `graph TB` mermaid block, the `Person:` prefix must NOT appear inside a rectangle-shape `[...]` node whose ID does not start with `actor`. Catches the rectangle sub-case of vocab inversion (`stripe["Person: Stripe API"]`, `system["Person: billing-service"]`) — per output-format.md actor-vocab contract, `Person:` labels actors only, and the canonical example IDs actor nodes as `actor` / `actor1` / etc. Regex-design rationale (testing-strategy.md §4 / #261): pattern uses ID-prefix discrimination (`(?!actor)` negative lookahead, case-insensitive) since shape alone cannot distinguish actor rectangle from system / external SaaS rectangle. The cylinder-shape opener `[(` is excluded via `\[(?!\()` so the cylinder sub-case (covered by sibling eval `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-cylinders`) does not double-fire here. Identifier-boundary lookbehind `(?<![A-Za-z0-9_-])` prevents matching mid-identifier. The `i` flag is global to the pattern: covers `Actor` capitalization drift on the ID side AND relaxes `Person:` to match `person:` / `PERSON:` — the latter is acceptable since non-canonical casing is itself a vocab violation worth catching. Anchored on the `### Context[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound per testing-strategy.md §3; the `(?:(?!```)[\s\S])*?` run between `graph TB` and the violation refuses to cross the closing ``` fence, so violations in later mermaid blocks (deps `graph LR`, data-flow `flowchart TD`) cannot leak into this assertion and false-ban a clean C4 block (closes #268). Known brittleness (both directions): false-positive on legitimate actor nodes using non-`actor*` IDs (e.g., `engineer1["Person: ..."]`) — intentional, surfaces convention drift on the canonical actor-ID prefix; false-negative on non-actor nodes whose IDs coincidentally start with `actor` (e.g., `actorOfNotaryService`) — documented miss, mitigated by future positive-bound rewrite (every `Person:` must co-occur with actor edge, not a fixed ID prefix). Negative twin to `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix` per testing-strategy.md §4.

evals.name: c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-id-less-rectangles
evals.summary: Negative twin (ID-less rectangle scope) to `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix`: the `Person:` prefix must NOT appear inside an ID-less rectangle-shape `["..."]` node under `### Context` per output-format.md actor-vocab contract — actor nodes are required to carry an `actor*` ID per the canonical convention, so any ID-less rectangle carrying `Person:` is an inversion. Closes #265 (testing-strategy.md §4 negative-twin convention). Followup to #261 (delivered via PR #264) — covers the syntactic shape that the ID-prefixed sibling eval (`c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-non-actor-rectangles`) cannot reach because its `[A-Za-z][\w-]*\[` segment requires a non-empty identifier before the rectangle opener.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-person-rect-idless-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: under the `### Context` header, within the C4 `graph TB` mermaid block, the `Person:` prefix must NOT appear inside an ID-less rectangle node (`["Person: …"]` declared without a leading identifier). Catches the ID-less sub-case of rectangle vocab inversion that the ID-prefixed sibling eval (`c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-non-actor-rectangles`) misses by construction — its `[A-Za-z][\w-]*\[` segment requires a non-empty identifier before the rectangle opener, so mermaid's permitted ID-less form (`graph TB\n  ["Person: ledger"]`) silently slips past. Regex-design rationale (testing-strategy.md §4 / #265): pre-`[` lookbehind `(?<![A-Za-z0-9_\]\)-])` excludes letters / digits / `_` / `-` (would be ID-prefixed rectangle, covered by sibling eval) plus `]` / `)` (would be edge syntax or cylinder closer). Whitespace, newline, and `>` (edge arrow tail) all pass — the latter intentional, an edge to an ID-less rectangle (`--> ["Person: …"]`) is the same vocab violation. The `\["` opener restricts to quoted-label rectangles; the cylinder opener `[(` is excluded by the `"` requirement, keeping the cylinder sub-case on its own eval (`c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-cylinders`). The unquoted ID-less form (`[Person: ledger]`) is covered by the followup sibling eval `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-unquoted-id-less-rectangles` (#269). The `i` flag is global to the pattern, relaxing `Person:` to `person:` / `PERSON:` since non-canonical casing is itself a vocab violation. Anchored on the `### Context[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound per testing-strategy.md §3; the `(?:(?!```)[\s\S])*?` run between `graph TB` and the violation refuses to cross the closing ``` fence, so violations in later mermaid blocks (deps `graph LR`, data-flow `flowchart TD`) cannot leak into this assertion and false-ban a clean C4 block (closes #268, applied uniformly across all four twins). Negative twin to `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix` per testing-strategy.md §4; followup sibling to `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-non-actor-rectangles` (#261), `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-cylinders` (#257), and `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-unquoted-id-less-rectangles` (#269).

evals.name: c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-unquoted-id-less-rectangles
evals.summary: Negative twin (unquoted ID-less rectangle scope) to `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix`: the `Person:` prefix must NOT appear inside an unquoted ID-less rectangle-shape `[...]` node under `### Context` per output-format.md actor-vocab contract — actor nodes are required to carry an `actor*` ID, so any unquoted ID-less rectangle carrying `Person:` is an inversion. Closes #269 (testing-strategy.md §4 negative-twin convention). Followup to #265 (delivered via PR #267) — covers the syntactic shape that the quoted ID-less sibling eval (`c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-id-less-rectangles`) cannot reach because its `\["` opener restricts to quoted-label rectangles, so mermaid's permitted unquoted single-word form (`graph TB\n  [Person: ledger]`) silently slips past.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-person-rect-unquoted-idless-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: under the `### Context` header, within the C4 `graph TB` mermaid block, the `Person:` prefix must NOT appear inside an unquoted ID-less rectangle node (`[Person: ledger]` declared without a leading identifier and without quotes). Catches the unquoted ID-less sub-case of rectangle vocab inversion that the quoted ID-less sibling eval (`c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-id-less-rectangles`) misses by construction — its `\["` opener requires a quote after the rectangle opener, so mermaid's permitted unquoted single-word form (`graph TB\n  [Person: ledger]`) silently slips past. Regex-design rationale (testing-strategy.md §4 / #269): pre-`[` lookbehind `(?<![A-Za-z0-9_\]\)-])` mirrors the quoted ID-less sibling — excludes letters / digits / `_` / `-` (would be ID-prefixed rectangle, covered by `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-non-actor-rectangles`) plus `]` / `)` (edge syntax or cylinder closer). Whitespace, newline, and `>` (edge-arrow tail) pass — `--> [Person: …]` is the same vocab violation. Post-`[` negative lookahead `(?!\(|")` excludes the cylinder opener `[(` (covered by `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-cylinders`) AND the quoted ID-less opener `["` (covered by `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-id-less-rectangles`), keeping each twin scoped to one syntactic shape. Mermaid in practice rejects unquoted labels containing whitespace, so multi-word inversions (`[Person: Platform engineer]`) cannot occur in this form; single-word inversions (`[Person: ledger]`, `[Person: stripe]`) remain reachable and are the regression target. Brittleness disclosure (mirrors sibling `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-non-actor-rectangles` at testing-strategy.md §4): false-negative direction inherits the §3 co-location bound — a violation appearing more than ~500 chars after `### Context` slips past, and a violation in label-syntax variants (`[Person: x]:::class`) still triggers since the regex stops at `Person:` before the closer; future positive-bound rewrite (every `Person:` must co-occur with an actor edge) would close both. Strategy chosen per #269: a fourth twin scoped to the unquoted opener, NOT widening the `\["?` opener on the quoted twin — the four-twin layout keeps each regex single-purpose and avoids fragile paired tightening of the cylinder exclusion. The `i` flag is global to the pattern, relaxing `Person:` to `person:` / `PERSON:` since non-canonical casing is itself a vocab violation. Anchored on the `### Context[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound per testing-strategy.md §3; the `(?:(?!```)[\s\S])*?` run between `graph TB` and the violation refuses to cross the closing ``` fence, so violations in later mermaid blocks (deps `graph LR`, data-flow `flowchart TD`) cannot leak into this assertion and false-ban a clean C4 block (closes #268, applied uniformly across all four twins). Negative twin to `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix` per testing-strategy.md §4; followup sibling to `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-id-less-rectangles` (#265), `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-non-actor-rectangles` (#261), and `c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-cylinders` (#257).

evals.name: c4-context-emits-non-actor-rectangle-presence
evals.summary: Positive-presence twin to the three `Person:`-not-on-non-actor-* negative twins (#257 / #261 / #265): at least one non-actor rectangle must appear inside the C4 `graph TB` block under `### Context`. Closes #266 — without this floor, a regression that omits the system-under-doc + external SaaS rectangles (leaving the actor as the only node) would pass every existing C4 assertion vacuously (negative twins assert absence-of-violation; positive `Person:` eval covers actor only; line-style eval can be satisfied by `inferred:` text alone if the lazy match leaks across blocks).
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-non-actor-rect-presence-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: under the `### Context` header, within the C4 `graph TB` mermaid block, at least one identifier-prefixed rectangle node whose ID does not start with `actor` must appear (system-under-doc, external SaaS, or sibling Module). Catches the actor-only regression class flagged in #266 — a skill change that emits only `actor1["Person: …"]` and omits the `system` / `stripe` rectangles entirely would pass every existing C4 assertion vacuously: `mermaid-c4-context-actor-uses-person-prefix` covers the actor node; the three `Person:`-not-on-non-actor-* negative twins (#257 / #261 / #265) assert absence-of-violation, which is trivially satisfied when no non-actor rectangles exist. Regex shape mirrors the ID-prefixed negative twin (`c4-context-person-prefix-not-on-non-actor-rectangles`, #261): `(?<![A-Za-z0-9_-])` identifier-boundary lookbehind, `(?!actor)` ID-prefix discrimination (case-insensitive), `\[(?!\()` rectangle-not-cylinder opener so a `postgres[(...)]` cylinder does not satisfy this floor (cylinders are datastores per output-format.md datastore-shape contract, not the system / external SaaS rectangles this floor targets). Anchored on the `### Context[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound per testing-strategy.md §3 with the fence-crossing bound `(?:(?!```)[\s\S])*?` per #268 so a non-actor rectangle in a later mermaid block (deps `graph LR`, data-flow `flowchart TD`) cannot satisfy this floor and false-pass an empty C4 block. Brittleness symmetric with the negative twin: a legitimate non-actor rectangle that coincidentally uses an `actor*` ID (e.g., `actorOfNotaryService`) would not satisfy this floor — same accepted miss documented on the negative twin. Positive-presence convention per testing-strategy.md §4 (positive twin to absence-of-violation negative twins; closes #266).

required: under the `### Context` header, within the C4 `graph TB` mermaid block, the `stripe[` rectangle node must appear. Fixture-specific deterministic floor: `ts-with-context/` exposes `STRIPE_API_KEY` in env without a `stripe` SDK in deps, which the `mermaid-c4-context-line-style-and-inferred-prefix` eval already requires the model to render as an inferred-edge target (`-.->` + `inferred:`). That eval can be satisfied by the textual tokens alone if the lazy match leaks across blocks; this assertion pins the specific node declaration so the regression mode in #266 (model emits the inferred-edge prose but omits the actual `stripe` node declaration) is caught directly. The cylinder opener `[(` is allowed by this pattern but mermaid forbids it for an external SaaS — the negative-twin cylinder eval (#257) catches the inversion. The identifier-boundary lookbehind `(?<![A-Za-z0-9_-])` prevents matching mid-identifier (e.g., `mystripe[`). Anchored on the `### Context[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound per testing-strategy.md §3; fence-crossing bound `(?:(?!```)[\s\S])*?` per #268 prevents leakage from a later mermaid block.

required: under the `### Context` header, within the C4 `graph TB` mermaid block, the `system[` rectangle node (the system-under-doc) must appear. Closes #266 review-followup gap: the generic non-actor rectangle floor in this eval is satisfied by ANY non-actor identifier-prefixed rectangle, so a regression that emits `stripe[` as the only non-actor rectangle and omits the system-under-doc would pass both the generic floor and the `stripe[` fixture pin. The canonical output-format.md example pins the system-under-doc node as `system["<repo-name>"]` (lines 58–62) — this assertion enforces that convention. Brittleness: model drift to a repo-name-derived ID (e.g., `billingservice[`, `billing_service[`) would false-negative — accepted miss, intentional. The miss surfaces convention drift on the canonical `system` ID prefix (parallel to the documented `actor*` ID-prefix brittleness on the negative-twin sibling #261). Identifier-boundary lookbehind `(?<![A-Za-z0-9_-])` prevents mid-identifier match (e.g., `subsystem[`). Anchored on the `### Context[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound per testing-strategy.md §3; fence-crossing bound `(?:(?!```)[\s\S])*?` per #268 prevents leakage from a later mermaid block.

evals.name: c4-context-no-banned-vocab-labels
evals.summary: Negative-vocab assertion: C4 Context block must not use `service:` / `component:` / `API:` as descriptive node-label vocabulary (closes #232 I-3, applies testing-strategy.md §4 negative-twin convention; ban list per output-format.md vocab-discipline contract).
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-with-context --output /tmp/arch-overview-c4-no-banned-vocab-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: under the `### Context` header, within the C4 `graph TB` mermaid block, banned descriptive vocabulary (`service:`, `component:`, `API:`) must NOT appear as node labels. Anchored on the `### Context[\s\S]{0,500}` co-location bound per testing-strategy.md §3 so post-block prose ('Stripe service: charges customers') and later mermaid blocks (deps `graph LR`, data-flow `flowchart TD`) cannot trigger a false ban on a clean C4 block. Literal proper nouns like `billing-service` and `Stripe API` are unaffected (no colon). Banned list mirrors output-format.md vocab-discipline contract exactly. Negative twin to uses-language-vocabulary per testing-strategy.md §4.

evals.name: skips-c4-context-when-actor-only
evals.summary: C4 Context emit floor is `(≥1 actor AND ≥1 adjacent)`. Fixture clears actor half (README names actor) but fails adjacent half (zero deps, zero env, zero I/O) → AND fails → skip path. Catches regression flipping `&&` to `||` (closes #254 problem 2 split-AND coverage).
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/actor-only --output /tmp/arch-overview-actor-only-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: response includes the canonical skip-note blockquote (`> _diagram skipped:`) — actor-only fixture trips the adjacent half of the AND floor. An OR-regression would emit the C4 block; this assertion fails on emit, catches the regression.

evals.name: skips-c4-context-when-adjacent-only
evals.summary: C4 Context emit floor is `(≥1 actor AND ≥1 adjacent)`. Fixture clears adjacent half (`pg` dep + `DATABASE_URL`) but fails actor half (no README → no actor signal) → AND fails → skip path. Catches regression flipping `&&` to `||` (closes #254 problem 2 split-AND coverage).
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/adjacent-only --output /tmp/arch-overview-adjacent-only-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: response includes the canonical skip-note blockquote (`> _diagram skipped:`) — adjacent-only fixture trips the actor half of the AND floor. An OR-regression would emit the C4 block; this assertion fails on emit, catches the regression.

evals.name: skips-c4-context-when-below-complexity-floor
evals.summary: Skill skips the inventory.md C4 Context block (with skip-note blockquote) when synthesis is below the sufficient-complexity floor — single isolated Module with no adjacent systems has nothing to render.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/empty --output /tmp/arch-overview-skip-c4-eval ; then show me what inventory.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: response includes the structured skip-note blockquote (`> _diagram skipped:`) when below complexity floor (no adjacent systems) — same prefix as deps + flow skip evals; tight match avoids false-positive on conversational mention

evals.name: skips-mermaid-graph-when-below-complexity-floor
evals.summary: Skill skips the dependencies.md graph LR block (with skip-note blockquote) when synthesis is below the sufficient-complexity floor — single-Module landscape has no Seam edges to render.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/no-manifest --output /tmp/arch-overview-skip-graph-eval ; then show me what dependencies.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation

required: response includes the structured skip-note blockquote (`> _diagram skipped:`) when below complexity floor (single-Module landscape) — tight match avoids false-positive on conversational mention

evals.summary: Skill skips the data-flow.md flowchart TD block (with skip-note blockquote) when lifecycle steps fall below the floor (≥3) — single-file fixture yields too few steps to render.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/no-manifest --output /tmp/arch-overview-skip-flow-eval ; then show me what data-flow.md contains

assertions.description: structural: skill fires on slash invocation
required: response includes the structured skip-note blockquote (`> _diagram skipped:`) when below complexity floor (sparse lifecycle)

evals.summary: Skill output applies LANGUAGE.md vocabulary (Module / Interface / Seam / Adapter) when describing the inventory.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/ts-only --output /tmp/arch-overview-vocab

assertions.description: structural: skill fires
diagnostic (per testing-strategy.md §6): pattern is broad seven-way OR of common software-engineering words — polish discipline, not spec acceptance. skill_invoked floor stays required.

evals.name: italic-marks-inferences
evals.summary: Skill response references the italic-marking convention for inferred claims.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/no-manifest --output /tmp/arch-overview-italic

assertions.description: structural: skill fires
diagnostic (per testing-strategy.md §6): pattern is broad three-way OR (`italic` | `inferred` | any `*…*` markdown) — polish discipline, not spec acceptance. skill_invoked floor stays required.

evals.name: refuses-output-inside-claude-config
evals.summary: Output guardrail: skill refuses to write the bundle inside the claude-config repo tree.
evals.prompt: /architecture-overview --repos tests/fixtures/architecture-overview/empty --output ./docs/test-bad-output

assertions.description: structural: skill fires
required: response surfaces the output-location guardrail
